title: Replace the quadratic spatial proximity scan
prompt: The proximity scanner in `src/proximity.cpp` is functionally correct, but the deterministic comparison-count harness shows that it computes a distance for every pair, even when points are far apart. Replace that O(n²) scan with a spatially selective implementation. Use `ScanStats::distance_comparisons` as the performance signal; do not introduce wall-clock timing or weaken the comparison budget. Preserve the public API and validation behavior, return every qualifying unordered pair exactly once, and keep results in the existing deterministic `(first, second)` order, including the order of equal-distance ties. Returned distances must remain the exact values produced by `std::hypot` for the original point pair rather than approximations or squared substitutes. For inputs of at most 32 points, preserve the existing all-pairs scan and its exact comparison counts so the optimization does not penalize tiny scans. Dense inputs may necessarily produce quadratically many results, but sparse large inputs must avoid quadratically many distance evaluations and must be spatially selective in both dimensions. Do not modify the protected tests, add dependencies, use network access, randomness, sleeps, or wall-clock measurements. Success is `python3 -B tests/run_tests.py` passing from the repository root.
